evals.expected_output: Reads the install journal, runs all eight checks (manifest, hook compile+import, canned-event dry-runs, HITL smoke, settings registration, git hook + dependencies, scorecard, agent-registry integrity) even after failures, writes .agentic/agentic-os/doctor.json, and reports a PASS/FAIL headline with a one-line remedy per failed check.

assertions.name: all-checks-run
assertions.description: All eight checks execute; an early failure does not short-circuit the report.

assertions.name: verdict-written
assertions.description: doctor.json is written with per-check passed/detail, failures, and pending_restart.

assertions.name: read-only
assertions.description: Nothing is fixed or modified besides doctor.json and the temporary Check-3 probe file, which is deleted.

evals.prompt: Run doctor. One managed hook in .claude/hooks/ contains an unrendered {{PLACEHOLDER}} and raises NameError when imported.
evals.expected_output: Check 2 fails the hook: py_compile may pass, but the import probe catches the NameError (and 2c catches the literal {{), so the doctor reports py_compile as failed with the offending hook named, and the overall verdict is FAIL.

assertions.name: import-not-just-compile
assertions.description: The hook is loaded as a module (guard checked first, never executed as __main__), so the NameError is caught even though py_compile succeeds.

assertions.name: fails-loudly
assertions.description: The verdict is FAIL and names the broken hook; the doctor does not attempt to repair it.

evals.prompt: Verify the agentic-os install. There is no .agentic/agentic-os/install.json in this repo.
evals.expected_output: Reports 'not installed — run /agentic-init' and writes a failed doctor.json containing only that finding, without running the other checks against a non-existent scaffold.

assertions.name: missing-journal-handled
assertions.description: The response states the repo is not installed and points at /agentic-init instead of failing with a traceback.
